0:99215.title: Common Billing Errors to Check
0:99215.citation: Billing for a more expensive procedure than what was performed. Example: Billing CPT 99215 (complex office visit, ~$250) when CPT 99213 (low-complexity visit, ~$110) was provided. Red flag: Charges seem disproportionate to the service received.
0:99215.why_it_applies: The charge of $316.00 for CPT 99215 (Level 5 complex office visit) is flagged as an overcharge and should be reviewed for potential upcoding — specifically whether the complexity of the visit actually warranted a Level 5 code rather than a lower-complexity visit code.

0:99215.title: How to Dispute a Medical Bill — Step-by-Step
0:99215.citation: Use CareGap's CMS PFS pricing tool to compare each CPT code's charge against the Medicare-allowed amount. A charge significantly above the Medicare rate (e.g., 3-5x or more) may be excessive.
0:99215.why_it_applies: The billed amount of $316.00 is 2.5x the Medicare-allowed rate of $125.13 for CPT 99215, which warrants a formal dispute through the provider's billing department and potentially the patient's insurer, BlueCross PPO, using the step-by-step dispute process outlined in this section.

letter.subject: Formal Dispute of Overcharge — Account #847291 — Jane M. Rodriguez — Date of Service 03/15/2024
letter.body:
Jane M. Rodriguez
[Your Address]

Date: [Today's Date]

Riverside Medical Associates
1200 Health Pkwy, Suite 300
Princeton, NJ 08544

Re: Formal Medical Bill Dispute
Patient Name: Jane M. Rodriguez
Date of Service: 03/15/2024
Account Number: 847291
Insurance: BlueCross PPO
Total Amount Billed: $748.00

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing on behalf of patient Jane M. Rodriguez to formally dispute a charge appearing on the above-referenced account. After a careful review of the itemized billing statement and a comparison against published Medicare Physician Fee Schedule rates, I have identified a charge that appears to be significantly disproportionate to the service rendered. I am requesting a thorough review, correction where appropriate, and written confirmation of the outcome within 30 days of receipt of this letter.

DISPUTED CHARGE

CPT Code 99215 — Office Visit, Level 5 (High-Complexity)
Amount Billed: $316.00
Medicare-Allowed Amount (2024 Physician Fee Schedule): $125.13
Multiplier: Approximately 2.5x the Medicare-allowed rate

A charge of $316.00 for a single office visit coded as CPT 99215 is materially inconsistent with established benchmarks. The Medicare-allowed amount for this code is $125.13 under the 2024 Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services Physician Fee Schedule. A charge that exceeds the Medicare rate by 2.5 times warrants scrutiny, particularly given that CPT 99215 is reserved for high-complexity medical decision-making involving extensive data review or a high risk of complications. If the visit provided did not meet the documentation and clinical criteria required to support a Level 5 designation, this charge may constitute upcoding, which is the practice of billing for a more complex or expensive service than was actually performed.

I respectfully request that your billing department review the clinical documentation for this encounter and confirm that the level of service billed is fully supported by the medical record, including the history, examination, and medical decision-making or total time documented by the treating provider.

REQUEST FOR ITEMIZED RECORDS

Pursuant to HIPAA, 45 C.F.R. Section 164.524, I am formally requesting a complete itemized statement of all charges associated with account number 847291 for the date of service 03/15/2024. This should include each CPT code billed, the corresponding charge, any adjustments applied, and the amounts submitted to and paid by BlueCross PPO. This request also includes any supporting clinical documentation used to determine the level of service assigned to CPT 99215.

APPLICABLE CONSIDERATIONS

While the No Surprises Act, effective January 1, 2022, primarily addresses surprise billing in the context of out-of-network services and emergency care, its broader framework reflects a federal policy commitment to billing transparency and patient protection. We note this context as relevant background to this dispute and to the expectation of good-faith engagement from your billing department.

REQUESTED RESOLUTION

We request that Riverside Medical Associates take the following actions within 30 days of receipt of this letter:

1. Conduct an internal audit of the CPT 99215 charge for the date of service 03/15/2024 and confirm in writing whether the level of service is supported by the clinical documentation.
2. If the documentation does not support a Level 5 visit, recode the charge to the appropriate CPT level and issue a corrected statement.
3. Provide the complete itemized bill and supporting documentation as described above under HIPAA 45 C.F.R. Section 164.524.
4. Provide written confirmation of the outcome of this review, including any adjustments made to the account balance.

NEXT STEPS IF UNRESOLVED

If a satisfactory written response is not received within 30 days of the date of this letter, or if the disputed charge is not corrected upon valid review, Jane M. Rodriguez reserves the right to escalate this matter through the following channels:

1. Filing a complaint with the New Jersey Office of the Attorney General, Division of Consumer Affairs.
2. Filing a complaint with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) regarding unfair or deceptive billing practices.
3. Filing a complaint with the New Jersey Department of Banking and Insurance regarding the handling of the BlueCross PPO claim associated with this account.
4. Reporting the potential upcoding concern to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services Office of Inspector General.

This letter is submitted in good faith and with the expectation of a professional and timely response. Please direct all correspondence related to this dispute to the address listed above.

Respectfully,

Jane M. Rodriguez
[Your Address]
Account Number: 847291
letter.recipient_block:
To:
Riverside Medical Associates
1200 Health Pkwy, Suite 300
Princeton, NJ 08544

roadmap.title: Request Itemized Bill with CPT Codes
roadmap.detail: Contact Riverside Medical Associates billing department and request a fully itemized bill listing every charge with its corresponding CPT code, date of service, and provider name. You are legally entitled to this document and it is the foundation for any dispute.

roadmap.title: Review Flagged Charge Against Medicare Rates
roadmap.detail: CareGap has already identified 1 flagged charge contributing to an estimated $190.87 in potential overcharges on your $748.00 bill. Cross-reference the specific line item on your itemized bill against the Medicare reimbursement rate for that CPT code to confirm the discrepancy before writing your dispute letter.

roadmap.title: Send a Formal Dispute Letter to Billing Department
roadmap.detail: Write a certified dispute letter to Riverside Medical Associates billing department citing the specific flagged charge, its CPT code, the amount billed versus the fair market or Medicare benchmark rate, and requesting a corrected bill or written justification. Send via certified mail with return receipt so you have a documented paper trail.

roadmap.title: File a Grievance with Your Insurance Provider
roadmap.detail: If you are insured, submit a formal grievance to your health insurance company referencing the same flagged charge and attach copies of your itemized bill and dispute letter. Insurers are often able to negotiate corrections directly with providers and may have contractual leverage Riverside Medical Associates must honor.

roadmap.title: File a Complaint with Your State Insurance Commissioner or Attorney General
roadmap.detail: If Riverside Medical Associates does not respond or refuses to correct the bill within 30 days, file a formal complaint with your state insurance commissioner and your state attorney general's consumer protection office, citing the specific billing error and your documented dispute attempts. Many states require providers to respond to complaints within 30 to 45 days.

roadmap.title: Consult a Medical Billing Advocate
roadmap.detail: If the dispute remains unresolved after regulatory complaints, consider engaging a certified medical billing advocate or patient advocate organization to negotiate on your behalf. Given the $190.87 in estimated savings, a billing advocate's fee is often offset by the recovered amount and they can identify additional errors you may have missed.

summary:
# Your Medical Bill Summary

You were charged $316 for a complex office visit, but Medicare says that same visit should only cost about $125—that's a difference of roughly $191 that might be overcharged. Your total bill from Riverside Medical Associates is $748, and we flagged this office visit charge as the main problem worth looking into. I'd recommend calling the billing department to ask why they charged so much more than the standard rate, or getting a second opinion from your insurance company about what you actually owe.
